unmatched_source_examples: Registration Citizens can register their complaints with police and then based on the evidence, facts and following investigation, police shall take the complaint forward.
Help Module The solution should provide detailed context-sensitive help material for all the possible actions and scenarios on all user interfaces in the application.
The solution should provide detailed context-sensitive help material for all the possible actions and scenarios on all user interfaces in the application.
The solution should send alerts (e.g., email, SMS) to the user if the user chooses to whenever any action has been taken on the alert.
The solution should enable the user to track the submitted defect or enhancement request.
The solution should enable the help-desk user to view the reports on the submitted defects or enhancement requests category-wise, status-wise, and age- wise.

The System must keep an unalterable audit trail capable of automatically capturing and storing information about: All the actions (create/read/update/delete) that are taken upon the critical entities (case, suspect, property,...) in the system The user initiating and or carrying out the action;
it may be subject to re-department and copying to removable media if required, so long as its contents remain unchanged.
Once the audit trail functionality has been activated, the System must track events without manual intervention, and store in the audit trail information about them.
The System must maintain the audit trail for as long as required, which will be at least for the life of the case to which it refers.
The System must at a minimum be able to provide reports for actions on cases organised: By case;
The System should be able to provide reports for actions on cases organised by workstation and (where technically appropriate) by network address.
The System must allow only admin-users to set up user profiles and allocate users to groups.
The System should allow a user to stipulate which other users or groups can access cases.

If a user requests access to, or searches for, a case which he does not have the right to access, the System must provide one of the following responses (selectable at configuration time): display title and metadata;
unmatched_generated_examples: For placing an order, we should streamline the process so that customers can add items to their cart, proceed to checkout with minimal steps, and complete payment without too many interactions.

For example, when a customer logs in and starts a new order, it should recognize them and auto-populate fields like shipping address and payment method where applicable.

It should be able to manage a list of items across different orders, so that if they start one order but then want to make another, they can easily switch between them without losing any items.
For example, when reading long articles or filling out forms, being able to increase the text size can make it much easier on the eyes and improve readability.
We should ensure that when users navigate through different sections of the application, they don't encounter issues like broken back button functionality or bookmarking problems.
Currently, the system can be a bit cumbersome because it has too many links that make the text hard to read at a glance.
We need to ensure that users can easily find help when they need it.
We need to ensure that users can easily understand where they are in the application.
For example, when a user is filling out a form for creating an account, it should clearly indicate which step they're on and how many steps remain.
For instance, screen readers should be able to navigate through the application smoothly without any issues.
If someone needs help with something specific, they should be able to access that information quickly without having to navigate away from their current task.
Each page should provide this information clearly and succinctly.
Currently, we've had issues with too many clicks required to get to certain sections, which can be frustrating for everyone but especially problematic for those with cognitive disabilities.

unmatched_source_examples: Operating Environment Web Store shall operate with the following internet browsers: Microsoft Internet Explorer version 6 and 7, Netscape Communicator Version 4 and 5.
Web Store shall operate on an Intel based system with Slackware Linux 2.6 and Apache Web Server.
Design and Implementation Constraints Must use a SQL based database.
User Documentation Shall install online help for users via the web interface Shall deliver Operations and Maintenance manual, Users Guide book, and Installation Instructions for the Administrator.

SI-1.3: Plug-ins interface Communications Interfaces CI-1: The WebOrder system shall send an e-mail confirmation to the customer that the items they ordered will be delivered to the shipping address along with tracking number.
Quality Attribute Requirements Upon the USB being plugged in the system shall be able to be deployed and operational in less than 1 minute.
The system shall be able to handle 1000 customers logged in concurrently at the same time.
The system shall be able to retrieve 200 products per second.
The system shall be able to validate credit card in less than 2 seconds.
The system shall be able to acquire shipping charges in less than 2 seconds.
Security Requirements The system shall validate credit cards against fraud.
The system shall encrypt all sensitive information via https.
The system shall encrypt all customer data in database.
The system shall be protected by open source firewall called Firestarter.
http://www.fs-security.com/ Availability Requirements The system shall have an availability of 99.99%.

Additionally, the system must support multiple browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge on both Windows and macOS operating systems.

unmatched_source_examples: DOCUMENT OVERVIEW This document outlines the software capabilities required for the XS components for the DII COE.
COE sponsors shall adhere to compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS.

In the fixed (static) mode of operation (base or data processing megacenter), the data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases.
The XS shall have the ability to redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances.
At a minimum, the session time-out values shall be user definable and be able to be reset prior to initialization of a user session.
End User Mode: Portion of XS services shall be used by various classes of users: data consumers, data and database managers, network information infrastructure resource managers.
Some of these uses of the data management services will entail unique requirements that shall be fulfilled within the capability of XS services.
In addition, maintenance also shall pertain to shutdown, open not-mounted and online/off-line implementations, modifications, upgrade, or other related actions.
The data management services shall support managing various types of data, database architectures and platforms that includes hardware and software at the specified sites.
In support of training activities, the data management services shall provide for the same processing as would be encountered in a production environment.
To contribute metadata to a registry, the producer must be able to receive XML registry forms, submit metadata and the related information resource artifacts, and notify.
DATA ACCESS SERVICES INTERNAL INTERFACE REQUIREMENTS The XS function shall provide standard APIs as specified in Appendix C.
The XS shall internally interface (transparent to the operator) with the existing data elements of the DBIF, DAC, and COTS RDBMS products.
Personnel requirements shall be determined by the developers of the system in which the XS module is embedded TRAINING-RELATED REQUIREMENTS Not applicable.
Training requirements shall be determined by the developers of the system in which the XS module is embedded.

The software should handle different types of user roles and permissions without conflicts.
The system should be able to scale efficiently and maintain performance under heavy load.
The software must ensure data encryption both in transit and at rest.
The system should be easily ported to other platforms if needed.
The software should handle different environments, including various browsers.
The system should be designed with a robust logging mechanism for debugging and auditing purposes.

unmatched_source_examples: The principles of the voucher, the contents of the health care services, their required quality, the standards to be met by the providers and their infrastructure, as well as the measures for monitoring the quality have been defined in the PDS.

This validity date can be amended or altered at the system level ONLY by the authorized user Voucher will also have MRP (maximum retail price) Voucher should have three tear off portioned slips with a sub-section tear-off for the partner.
The values of Health Sub – District, Locality, Level Of Facility, Type of facility, Registration Body would be list from their own master and should select the details based on the VSP.
The VSP Master Information screen should have a provision to enter the payment terms agreed between MSIU and VSP.

VSP Name Staff Code Staff Name Staff Type Qualification The system will generate message while creating a new staff with existing name, but the system will allow the user to save that new staff if its required.
Claim (Treatment Form) Submission Depends on the payment terms (Bi-monthly / Monthly) mentioned in the VSP master the VSP should submit the Claim (Treatment form) to the MISU Field office.
These information is vital and shall be used for compared with the processed claims.
The document should have the following information.
Before the claim entry the user should check the form manually to understand whether any mandatory information is missed in the Claim or not.
If yes, then the user should mark that Claim (Treatment Form) status as Rejected and send back to VSP.

Patient Status - (Select from list of status options populating from Patient Status Master) suppose the status is Referral then the system will have the provision to capture Referred To and Reason for Referral, the reason for Referral should be populated from Referral master.

If the claim from same VSP having more than two times of Thump Impression mismatching, then the system should automatically produce a warning message and the same time the system should In-Activate the VSP.
The system admin only can able to create User Groups The system should have the provision to create any number of individual user under any user group.
